source.title: Temu Warehouse Locations: 2026 Directory of US, Europe & Global Distribution Centers

extractions.claim: Temu operated 13 self-owned warehouses globally by October 2025, with plans for a 14th in Brazil — a massive infrastructure buildout from zero in 2022
extractions.evidence: By October 2025, Temu operated 13 self-owned warehouses globally: 10 in Europe, 2 in the United States, and 1 near the US-Mexico border. Notable examples: 120,000 sqm 'Jitu Warehouse US-Mexico', 165,000 sqm logistics center in Gimpo, South Korea. A 14th warehouse project planned for Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.

verification.verifier_notes: From AMZPrep 2026 directory article. Warehouse counts and sizes cross-referenced with multiple logistics industry sources.

extractions.claim: Temu's US warehouse strategy pivoted from air-freight-direct to local fulfillment after the de minimis exemption ended in April 2025
extractions.evidence: In April 2025, the US de minimis exemption ended, meaning small-value imports were no longer tariff-free. Temu pivoted by onboarding US-based sellers, investing in regional warehouses for faster, tariff-compliant fulfillment. Temu's California warehouse (Rowland Heights) serves as West Coast hub; Dallas as central US hub. Temu cooperates with WINIT and Export for additional US warehousing capacity.

verification.verifier_notes: De minimis end date and Temu pivot confirmed by multiple sources. US warehouse locations from AMZPrep and logistics industry articles.

extractions.claim: Temu's European warehouses are projected to handle 80% of European orders domestically by 2026, fundamentally changing the cost structure
extractions.evidence: Warehouses in Rotterdam, Frankfurt, UK, Germany, Austria, Italy, Spain, and France. Projected to handle 80% of European orders domestically by 2026. EU duty reform starting July 1, 2026 will remove the EUR 150 duty-free threshold entirely, imposing a mandatory fee per item.

verification.verifier_notes: 80% domestic fulfillment projection is from Cross-Border Magazine forecast, not confirmed by PDD. EU duty reform date is from regulatory announcements.

extractions.claim: Chinese 3PL operators and e-commerce companies now account for 20% of new US warehouse leasing, reflecting the scale of the buildout
extractions.evidence: According to Prologis, as of Q3 2025, Chinese 3PL operators and e-commerce companies accounted for 20% of the net leasing of new warehouse space in the US, a share that has risen sharply in recent years.

verification.verifier_notes: Prologis is a primary source as the largest industrial REIT globally. This 20% figure reflects Temu + Shein + other Chinese cross-border operators.
